title: CONTENT FROM THE INTELLIGENT INVESTOR Last year, Alerion launched a three-year I...
content:
CONTENT FROM THE INTELLIGENT INVESTOR Last year, Alerion launched a three-year Industrial Plan with the goal of doubling the company’s installed capacity to approximately 2.5 GW by 2028. The company is also active in further expanding its European footprint through a combination of organic growth and the reinvestment of proceeds from the sale of existing investments back into new portfolio companies. Sixty-five percent of the plants will be in Italy and 30% in Romania, with a technological mix dominated by wind power (80%) and photovoltaic power (20%). Up to $450 million of the $2.1 billion of the required investment has or is being raised through Alerion’s Green Bonds. The plan is already on track to nearly double Alerion’s EBITDA from $232 million in 2025, to more than $440 million by the end of the 2028 financial year. Not surprisingly, investors have been queueing. Investor enthusiasm is entirely logical, given that Alerion is a demonstrably well-run company with a winning combination of vision, drive	Mr. Josef Gostner - CEO and President of ALERION and flexibility. On top of that, though, it is also very much a company in the right place
